name: agents-writing-skills
description: Skills and prompts for agents that write text without sounding like AI (v1.3: 3-pass architecture + 13 worked examples + benchmark script + bias substitution check)

skills.name: humanize-writer
skills.description: Write new prose without AI tells (3-pass architecture: Audit → Rewrite → Verify)
skills.use_when: User asks to write documentation, README, blog post, email, or any narrative text
skills.changelog: v5: 3-pass architecture (Audit → Rewrite → Verify). 12 levers organized into 4 phases: STRIP (1-9) / TIGHTEN (10) / RELY (11) / REBUILD (12). Decision tree, multi-language support table, code-block examples. v4: Lever 12 + length bias research + bias substitution warning.

skills.name: humanize-editor
skills.description: Rewrite existing text to read human (3-pass: Audit → Rewrite → Verify, with bias substitution check)
skills.use_when: User asks to humanize, de-AI, rewrite AI-generated text, or shorten verbose text
skills.changelog: v5: 3-pass architecture matches humanize-writer. Step 4.4 bias substitution check (Lamparth 2026): факты не потеряны при сокращении. Step 4 reorganized as STRIP/TIGHTEN/RELY/REBUILD phases. Voice profile 'laconic' added.

skills.name: anti-ai-auditor
skills.description: Diagnose AI patterns without rewriting (3-pass audit: Surface → Deep → Synthesis)
skills.use_when: User asks to check if text sounds like AI, asks 'is this too long?', or wants a diagnosis only
skills.changelog: v4: 3-pass audit architecture (Surface Scan → Deep Analysis → Synthesis). Bias substitution check integrated. Voice, format-bias, Russian-grammar opportunities as independent analysis passes.

skills.name: ai-pattern-rewriter
skills.description: Fix specific AI-pattern phrases surgically (3-pass: Identify → Rewrite → Verify)
skills.use_when: User identifies specific phrases that sound too AI or 'too long' and wants targeted fixes
skills.changelog: v4: 3-pass surgical architecture (Identify → Rewrite → Verify). Per-span bias substitution check. Over-generation spans + Russian brevity grammar spans + Williams 6 ops as integrated catalog.

knowledge_base.description: Obsidian-format knowledge vault. All SKILL.md files reference this repo via GitHub URLs (github.com/.../blob/main/knowledge/...). For offline use, install via scripts/install-knowledge.sh.

structure.06-Sources/: Raw research papers and web fetches used to build the knowledge

install_instructions.opencode: Copy each skill directory to ~/.config/opencode/skills/. Each skill is a single SKILL.md file with optional references/ subdirectory. Knowledge base references resolve via GitHub URLs by default. For offline use: ./scripts/install-knowledge.sh.
install_instructions.pi: Copy each skill directory to ~/.pi/agent/skills/. Copy prompts to ~/.pi/agent/prompts/. Knowledge base references resolve via GitHub URLs by default. For offline use: ./scripts/install-knowledge.sh.
install_instructions.claude_code: Copy each skill directory to ~/.claude/skills/ or use the /install-skill command. Knowledge base references resolve via GitHub URLs by default. For offline use: ./scripts/install-knowledge.sh.
install_instructions.generic: Read this manifest, identify which skills you want, and copy the SKILL.md files to your agent's skill directory. Knowledge base references are GitHub URLs (https://github.com/11111000000/agents-writing-skills/blob/main/knowledge/...). For offline use, run scripts/install-knowledge.sh to clone the knowledge base locally.
